content.title: What is C Programming?
content.content:
C is a powerful general-purpose programming language created by Dennis Ritchie at Bell Laboratories in 1972. It's one of the most widely used programming languages and has influenced many other languages like C++, Java, and Python.

C is known for its efficiency, portability, and ability to work close to the hardware, making it ideal for system programming, embedded systems, and performance-critical applications.

content.title: Why Learn C?
content.content:
• Foundation for Other Languages - Understanding C helps you learn other languages faster
• System Programming - Essential for operating systems and device drivers
• Performance - Direct hardware access and efficient memory management
• Portability - Code can run on different platforms with minimal changes
• Industry Standard - Still widely used in embedded systems and legacy systems

content.title: Your First C Program
Variables and Data Types
content.content:
C provides several basic data types:

• int - Integer numbers
• float - Single precision floating point
• double - Double precision floating point
• char - Single character
• void - Represents absence of type

Variables must be declared before use, specifying their type and name.

content.title: Variable Declaration Example
Decision Making
content.content:
C provides several control structures:

1. if-else statements
2. switch-case statements
3. Conditional (ternary) operator
4. Logical operators (&&, ||, !)

These structures help control the flow of program execution based on conditions.
